title: PRISMA flow diagrams with ggconsort
text: real review, start data frame citation records exported reference manager, one row per record columns track record’s fate. simulate one.
code: library(ggconsort) library(dplyr)  citations <- tibble(id = 1:500) %>%   mutate(     source = if_else(id <= 412, "Databases", "Registers"),     duplicate = id <= 102,     passed_screen = !duplicate & id > 353,     retrieved = passed_screen & id > 362,     exclusion_reason = case_when(       retrieved & id <= 409 ~ "Wrong population",       retrieved & id <= 448 ~ "Wrong outcome",       retrieved & id <= 469 ~ "Wrong study design",       TRUE ~ NA_character_     )   )  head(citations) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>      id source    duplicate passed_screen retrieved exclusion_reason #>   <int> <chr>     <lgl>     <lgl>         <lgl>     <chr>            #> 1     1 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA               #> 2     2 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA               #> 3     3 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA               #> 4     4 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA               #> 5     5 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA               #> 6     6 Databases TRUE      FALSE         FALSE     NA

title: PRISMA flow diagrams with ggconsort
text: PRISMA box cohort. CONSORT workflow, cohort_define() derives cohort full set records previously defined cohort, anti_join() convenient way count records drop step.
code: review_cohorts <-   citations %>%   cohort_start("Records identified") %>%   cohort_define(     from_databases = .full %>% filter(source == "Databases"),     from_registers = .full %>% filter(source == "Registers"),     screened = .full %>% filter(!duplicate),     duplicates = anti_join(.full, screened, by = "id"),     sought = screened %>% filter(passed_screen),     screened_out = anti_join(screened, sought, by = "id"),     assessed = sought %>% filter(retrieved),     not_retrieved = anti_join(sought, assessed, by = "id"),     included = assessed %>% filter(is.na(exclusion_reason)),     excluded_population = assessed %>% filter(exclusion_reason == "Wrong population"),     excluded_outcome = assessed %>% filter(exclusion_reason == "Wrong outcome"),     excluded_design = assessed %>% filter(exclusion_reason == "Wrong study design")   ) %>%   cohort_label(     from_databases = "Databases",     from_registers = "Registers",     screened = "Records screened",     duplicates = "Duplicate records removed",     sought = "Reports sought for retrieval",     screened_out = "Records excluded",     assessed = "Reports assessed for eligibility",     not_retrieved = "Reports not retrieved",     included = "Studies included in review",     excluded_population = "Wrong population",     excluded_outcome = "Wrong outcome",     excluded_design = "Wrong study design"   )  review_cohorts #> A ggconsort cohort of 500 observations with 12 cohorts: #>   - from_databases (412) #>   - from_registers (88) #>   - screened (398) #>   - duplicates (102) #>   - sought (147) #>   - screened_out (251) #>   - assessed (138) #>   - not_retrieved (9) #>   ...and 4 more.

title: PRISMA flow diagrams with ggconsort
text:
PRISMA box declares row col grid position: main flow runs "main" column, reasons attrition beside "side" column. Arrows connect boxes name — boxes column joined vertically boxes row horizontally, PRISMA-style, box edge box edge. stage labels left consort_stage_add() badges; row = c(2, 4) centers “Screening” across rows. Multi-line labels built
(labels rendered gridtext, markdown HTML formatting work). ggconsort measures every box plot drawn computes spacing fit figure, coordinates needed anywhere. analysis time, included studies one cohort_pull() away:
code:
review_prisma <- review_cohorts %>% consort_box_add( "identified", row = 1, label = glue::glue( "Records identified from:
{cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, from_databases)}
{cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, from_registers)}" ) ) %>% consort_box_add( "duplicates", row = 1, col = "side", label = glue::glue( "Records removed before screening:
{cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, duplicates)}" ) ) %>% consort_box_add( "screened", row = 2, label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, screened) ) %>% consort_box_add( "screened_out", row = 2, col = "side", label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, screened_out) ) %>% consort_box_add( "sought", row = 3, label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, sought) ) %>% consort_box_add( "not_retrieved", row = 3, col = "side", label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, not_retrieved) ) %>% consort_box_add( "assessed", row = 4, label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, assessed) ) %>% consort_box_add( "excluded", row = 4, col = "side", label = glue::glue( "Reports excluded:
• {cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, excluded_population)}
• {cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, excluded_outcome)}
• {cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, excluded_design)}" ) ) %>% consort_box_add( "included", row = 5, label = cohort_count_adorn(review_cohorts, included) ) %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "identified", end = "screened") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "screened", end = "sought") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "sought", end = "assessed") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "assessed", end = "included") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "identified", end = "duplicates") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "screened", end = "screened_out") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "sought", end = "not_retrieved") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "assessed", end = "excluded") %>% consort_stage_add("Identification", row = 1, angle = 90) %>% consort_stage_add("Screening", row = c(2, 4), angle = 90) %>% consort_stage_add("Included", row = 5, angle = 90) library(ggplot2) review_prisma %>% ggplot() + geom_consort() + theme_consort() review_cohorts %>% cohort_pull(included) #> # A tibble: 31 × 6 #> id source duplicate passed_screen retrieved exclusion_reason #> #> 1 470 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 2 471 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 3 472 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 4 473 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 5 474 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 6 475 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 7 476 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 8 477 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 9 478 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> 10 479 Registers FALSE TRUE TRUE NA #> # ℹ 21 more rows

code: @Manual{,   title = {ggconsort: Create CONSORT Diagrams},   author = {Travis Gerke and Garrick Aden-Buie},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://tgerke.github.io/ggconsort}, }

title: Create CONSORT Diagrams
text: goal ggconsort provide convenience functions creating CONSORT diagrams ggplot2. ggconsort segments CONSORT creation two stages: (1) counting annotation time data wrangling, (2) diagram layout aesthetic design. introduction ggconsort_cohort class, stage (1) can accomplished within dplyr chains. Specifically, following functions implemented inside dplyr chain define ggconsort_cohort: cohort_start() initializes ggconsort_cohort object contains labeled copy source data cohort_define() constructs cohorts variations source data cohorts cohort_label() adds labels named cohort within ggconsort_cohort object Stage 2 makes use three ggconsort consort_ verbs equip ggconsort_cohort object ggconsort properties. ggconsort object can viewed ggplot via geom_consort() + theme_consort(). plot print methods also available ggconsort object visually iterative development. consort_box_add() adds text box CONSORT diagram, row/col grid position (laid automatically draw time) explicit coordinates consort_arrow_add() adds arrow CONSORT diagram consort_line_add() adds line (without arrow head) CONSORT diagram consort_stage_add() adds stage badge (e.g. “Allocation”) row/column layout

title: Create CONSORT Diagrams
text: can install released version ggconsort GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("tgerke/ggconsort")

title: Create CONSORT Diagrams
text: demonstrate usage, use simulated dataset within ggconsort called trial_data, contains 1200 patients approached participate randomized trial comparing Drug Drug B. 1200 approached patients, subset ultimately randomized: declined participate ineligible (due prior chemotherapy bone metastasis). use ggconsort verbs geoms count patient flow represent process CONSORT diagram. first define ggconsort_cohort object (study_cohorts) following dplyr chain. can look study_cohorts object print summary methods: Next, lay diagram. Boxes declare row col grid position instead coordinates: col = "main" (default) central spine, "side" puts box right, numbers give finer control (arms sit columns -1 1). Arrows just connect boxes name — vertically within column, horizontally within row, end = c("arm_a", "arm_b") draws T-split study arms. ggconsort measures every box plot drawn spaces grid fit device, coordinates fiddle nothing gets clipped. Note also cohort_count_adorn(), convenience function glues cohort counts labels.  Prefer place things hand? consort_box_add() consort_arrow_add() also accept explicit coordinates — see ?consort_box_add. point, ready analysis. following retrieves desired data frame randomized subjects:
code:
library(ggconsort) head(trial_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #> id declined prior_chemo bone_mets treatment #> #> 1 65464 0 0 0 Drug A #> 2 48228 0 0 0 Drug B #> 3 92586 0 0 0 Drug A #> 4 70176 0 0 0 Drug B #> 5 89052 0 0 0 Drug A #> 6 97333 0 0 0 Drug B library(dplyr) study_cohorts <- trial_data %>% cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>% # Define cohorts using named expressions -------------------- # Notice that you can use previously defined cohorts in subsequent steps cohort_define( consented = .full %>% filter(declined != 1), consented_chemonaive = consented %>% filter(prior_chemo != 1), randomized = consented_chemonaive %>% filter(bone_mets != 1), treatment_a = randomized %>% filter(treatment == "Drug A"), treatment_b = randomized %>% filter(treatment == "Drug B"), # anti_join is useful for counting exclusions ------------- excluded = anti_join(.full, randomized, by = "id"), excluded_declined = anti_join(.full, consented, by = "id"), excluded_chemo = anti_join(consented, consented_chemonaive, by = "id"), excluded_mets = anti_join(consented_chemonaive, randomized, by = "id") ) %>% # Provide text labels for cohorts --------------------------- cohort_label( consented = "Consented", consented_chemonaive = "Chemotherapy naive", randomized = "Randomized", treatment_a = "Allocated to arm A", treatment_b = "Allocated to arm B", excluded = "Excluded", excluded_declined = "Declined to participate", excluded_chemo = "Prior chemotherapy", excluded_mets = "Bone metastasis" ) study_cohorts #> A ggconsort cohort of 1200 observations with 9 cohorts: #> - consented (1141) #> - consented_chemonaive (1028) #> - randomized (938) #> - treatment_a (469) #> - treatment_b (469) #> - excluded (262) #> - excluded_declined (59) #> - excluded_chemo (113) #> ...and 1 more. summary(study_cohorts) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #> cohort count label #> #> 1 .full 1200 Assessed for eligibility #> 2 consented 1141 Consented #> 3 consented_chemonaive 1028 Chemotherapy naive #> 4 randomized 938 Randomized #> 5 treatment_a 469 Allocated to arm A #> 6 treatment_b 469 Allocated to arm B #> 7 excluded 262 Excluded #> 8 excluded_declined 59 Declined to participate #> 9 excluded_chemo 113 Prior chemotherapy #> 10 excluded_mets 90 Bone metastasis library(ggplot2) study_consort <- study_cohorts %>% consort_box_add( "full", row = 1, label = cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, .full) ) %>% consort_box_add( "exclusions", row = 2, col = "side", label = glue::glue( '{cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, excluded)}
• {cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, excluded_declined)}
• {cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, excluded_chemo)}
• {cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, excluded_mets)} ') ) %>% consort_box_add( "randomized", row = 3, label = cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, randomized) ) %>% consort_box_add( "arm_a", row = 4, col = -1, label = cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, treatment_a) ) %>% consort_box_add( "arm_b", row = 4, col = 1, label = cohort_count_adorn(study_cohorts, treatment_b) ) %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "full", end = "randomized") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "full", end = "exclusions") %>% consort_arrow_add(start = "randomized", end = c("arm_a", "arm_b")) %>% consort_stage_add("Allocation", row = 4, col = "main") study_consort %>% ggplot() + geom_consort() + theme_consort() study_cohorts %>% cohort_pull(randomized) #> # A tibble: 938 × 5 #> id declined prior_chemo bone_mets treatment #> #> 1 65464 0 0 0 Drug A #> 2 48228 0 0 0 Drug B #> 3 92586 0 0 0 Drug A #> 4 70176 0 0 0 Drug B #> 5 89052 0 0 0 Drug A #> 6 97333 0 0 0 Drug B #> 7 80724 0 0 0 Drug A #> 8 65186 0 0 0 Drug B #> 9 48837 0 0 0 Drug A #> 10 99005 0 0 0 Drug B #> # ℹ 928 more rows

what: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
text: Count number rows ggconsort cohort

title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
code: cohort_count(.data, ...)  cohort_count_int(.data, ...)  cohort_count_adorn(.data, ..., .label_fn = NULL)

title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
text: .data ggconsort_cohort object ... Cohorts include output, can quoted unquoted cohort names, tidyselect helpers tidyselect::starts_with(). .label_fn optional custom function formatting cohort counts. called per cohort arguments label, count, cohort, return single string.

title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
text: tibble cohort name, row number total, label.

title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
text: cohort_count(): Returns tibble cohort name, row number total label. cohort_count_int(): Returns named vector cohort counts. cohort_count_adorn(): Returns cohort count "(n = )" custom format. default, counts formatted comma separators, e.g. "Randomized (n = 5,932,291)".

title: Count the number of rows in each ggconsort cohort — cohort_count
code: cohorts <- trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%     cohort_define(       consented = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1),       consented_chemonaive = consented %>% dplyr::filter(prior_chemo != 1)     ) %>%     cohort_label(       consented = "Consented",       consented_chemonaive = "Chemotherapy naive"     )  cohorts %>%   cohort_count() #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   cohort               count label                    #>   <chr>                <int> <chr>                    #> 1 .full                 1200 Assessed for eligibility #> 2 consented             1141 Consented                #> 3 consented_chemonaive  1028 Chemotherapy naive        cohorts %>%   cohort_count_adorn() #> [1] "Assessed for eligibility (n = 1,200)" #> [2] "Consented (n = 1,141)"                #> [3] "Chemotherapy naive (n = 1,028)"        cohorts %>%   cohort_count_adorn(     starts_with("consented"),     .label_fn = function(cohort, label, count, ...) {       glue::glue("{count} {label} ({cohort})")     }   ) #> [1] "1141 Consented (consented)"                     #> [2] "1028 Chemotherapy naive (consented_chemonaive)"

code: cohorts <- trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%     cohort_define(       consented = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1),       consented_chemonaive = consented %>% dplyr::filter(prior_chemo != 1)     )  cohorts %>% cohort_pull(consented_chemonaive) #> # A tibble: 1,028 × 5 #>       id declined prior_chemo bone_mets treatment #>    <int>    <int>       <int>     <int> <chr>     #>  1 65464        0           0         0 Drug A    #>  2 48228        0           0         0 Drug B    #>  3 92586        0           0         0 Drug A    #>  4 70176        0           0         0 Drug B    #>  5 89052        0           0         0 Drug A    #>  6 97333        0           0         0 Drug B    #>  7 80724        0           0         0 Drug A    #>  8 65186        0           0         0 Drug B    #>  9 48837        0           0         0 Drug A    #> 10 99005        0           0         0 Drug B    #> # ℹ 1,018 more rows

code: cohorts <-   trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%   cohort_define(     consented = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1),     treatment_a = consented %>% dplyr::filter(treatment == "Drug A"),     treatment_b = consented %>% dplyr::filter(treatment == "Drug B")   ) %>%   cohort_label(     consented = "Consented",     treatment_a = "Allocated to arm A",     treatment_b = "Allocated to arm B"   )  cohorts %>% cohort_select(consented) #> A ggconsort cohort of 1200 observations with 1 cohort: #>   - consented (1141)  cohorts %>%   cohort_select(starts_with("treatment_")) #> A ggconsort cohort of 1200 observations with 2 cohorts: #>   - treatment_a (469) #>   - treatment_b (469)

what: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
text: functions add visual elements CONSORT diagram ggconsort_cohort object, converting ggconsort object can plotted ggplot2::ggplot() geom_consort().

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
code: consort_arrow_add(   .data,   start = NA,   start_side = NA,   end = NA,   end_side = NA,   start_x = NA,   start_y = NA,   end_x = NA,   end_y = NA )  consort_box_add(   .data,   name,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   label,   hjust = NULL,   vjust = NULL,   row = NULL,   col = NULL )  consort_stage_add(.data, label, row, col = "left", fill = "#9bc0fc", angle = 0)  consort_line_add(   .data,   start = NA,   start_side = NA,   end = NA,   end_side = NA,   start_x = NA,   start_y = NA,   end_x = NA,   end_y = NA )

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
text:
.data ggconsort_cohort object, ggconsort object returned previous consort_*_add() call. start, end Names boxes arrow line starts ends. row/column layout, end may vector names draw T-split start several boxes. start_side, end_side side box ("left", "right", "top", "bottom") arrow line leaves points . Optional: default sides follow relative positions two boxes. start_x, start_y, end_x, end_y Explicit coordinates start end arrow line, alternative naming boxes start/end. available row/column layouts. name character name identifying box, used connect arrows lines box. x, y Coordinates box. Omit (set row/col) use row/column layout instead. label Text displayed box. Interpreted markdown/HTML ggtext/gridtext, labels may contain formatting
**bold**. cohort_count_adorn() convenient way build labels cohort counts. hjust, vjust Optional numeric justification box relative (x, y), [0, 1]. default boxes centered coordinates (hjust = vjust = 0.5). E.g. hjust = 0 places box's left edge x. Ignored row/column layouts. row, col Grid position box stage badge (see "Row/column layout" section). consort_stage_add(), row may length-2 vector center badge across span rows. fill Fill color stage badge. angle Rotation stage badge text, degrees (e.g. 90 vertical labels PRISMA diagram).

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
text: ggconsort object.

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
text: consort_box_add() adds text box, either explicit (x, y) coordinates (row, col) grid position. consort_arrow_add() adds arrow two boxes (referenced name) explicit coordinates. consort_line_add() adds line without arrow head, interface consort_arrow_add(). consort_stage_add() adds stage badge (e.g. "Allocation") row/column layout.

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
text: Instead picking coordinates hand, boxes can declare grid position row col. Rows count 1 top. col "main" (central spine, column 0), "side" (column 1, right spine), "left" (column -1), number. draw time ggconsort measures every box open graphics device lays grid fill plot: row gaps equalized, columns spread boxes never overlap, nothing clipped, whatever device size. row/column layout, arrows need start end names: boxes column connected vertically, boxes row connected horizontally, box another column row reached horizontal branch start box's vertical spine, height target box, vector end (e.g. end = c("arm_a", "arm_b")) draws classic T-split: one drop start box, crossbar, arrow arm. diagram must use either coordinates rows/columns, mixture.

title: Add boxes, arrows, lines, and stage badges to a CONSORT diagram — consort_arrow_add
code: cohorts <- trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%   cohort_define(     randomized = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1),     excluded = dplyr::anti_join(.full, randomized, by = "id")   ) %>%   cohort_label(     randomized = "Randomized",     excluded = "Declined to participate"   )  # row/column layout: no coordinates, spacing computed at draw time consort <- cohorts %>%   consort_box_add("full", row = 1, col = "main",     label = cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, .full)) %>%   consort_box_add("exclusions", row = 2, col = "side",     label = cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, excluded)) %>%   consort_box_add("randomized", row = 3, col = "main",     label = cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, randomized)) %>%   consort_arrow_add(start = "full", end = "randomized") %>%   consort_arrow_add(start = "full", end = "exclusions")  library(ggplot2) ggplot(consort) +   geom_consort() +   theme_consort()

what: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
title: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
text: geom_consort() draws boxes, arrows, lines ggconsort object created consort_box_add(), consort_arrow_add(), consort_line_add(). measures rendered size box draw time, boxes centered (x, y) coordinates arrows start end exactly box edges, whatever device size. geom_consort_arrow() geom_consort_box() legacy component layers, anchor arrows box centers shift box text justification; remain available diagrams depend behavior. geom_consort_line() draws standalone line segment styling diagram lines.

title: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
code: geom_consort(   label_color = "black",   label_size = 11,   label_height = 1,   fill = "white",   box_color = "black" )  geom_consort_arrow(...)  geom_consort_box(label_color = "black", label_size = 11, label_height = 1, ...)  geom_consort_line(x, xend, y, yend, ...)

title: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
text: label_color Color box label text. label_size Size box label text, points. label_height Line height box label text. fill Fill color boxes. box_color Color box borders. ... geom_consort_box(), additional arguments passed ggtext::geom_richtext(). Ignored geom_consort_arrow(). x, xend, y, yend Coordinates line segment drawn geom_consort_line().

title: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
text: ggplot2 layer list layers can added plot.

title: CONSORT diagram layers for ggplot2 — geom_consort
code: cohorts <- trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%   cohort_define(     randomized = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1)   ) %>%   cohort_label(randomized = "Randomized")  consort <- cohorts %>%   consort_box_add("full", 0, 10, cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, .full)) %>%   consort_box_add("randomized", 0, 0, cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, randomized)) %>%   consort_arrow_add("full", "bottom", "randomized", "top")  library(ggplot2) ggplot(consort) +   geom_consort() +   theme_consort(margin_h = 12, margin_v = 5)

what: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
title: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
text: wrapper around ggplot2::theme_void() adds plot margins. Margins give diagrams explicit x/y coordinates breathing room boxes near edges clipped; row/column layouts size device rarely need .

title: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
code: theme_consort(margin_h = 0, margin_v = 0, margin_unit = "line")

title: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
text: margin_h, margin_v Horizontal vertical plot margins. margin_unit Unit margins, passed ggplot2::margin().

title: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: Minimal theme for CONSORT diagrams — theme_consort
code: cohorts <- trial_data %>%   cohort_start("Assessed for eligibility") %>%   cohort_define(     randomized = .full %>% dplyr::filter(declined != 1)   ) %>%   cohort_label(randomized = "Randomized")  consort <- cohorts %>%   consort_box_add("full", row = 1, label = cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, .full)) %>%   consort_box_add("randomized", row = 2, label = cohort_count_adorn(cohorts, randomized)) %>%   consort_arrow_add(start = "full", end = "randomized")  library(ggplot2) ggplot(consort) +   geom_consort() +   theme_consort()

title: ggconsort 0.3.0
text: consort_box_add() gains row/column layout: declare row col ("main", "side", "left", number) instead x/y coordinates, diagram laid draw time measured box sizes — row gaps equalized, columns spread nothing overlaps clips, layout adapts device size. Explicit coordinates remain fully supported. row/column layout, consort_arrow_add() needs start end box names: boxes column connect vertically, boxes row horizontally, boxes elsewhere branch start box’s column. vector end (e.g. end = c("arm_a", "arm_b")) draws classic T-split study arms one call. New consort_stage_add() adds stage badges (“Allocation”, “Identification”, …) row/column layout, vertically centered row span rows, optional rotation (angle = 90) PRISMA-style margin labels. geom_consort() now single layer measures box open graphics device draw time. Boxes placed exactly (x, y) coordinates (centered default, per user-supplied hjust/vjust), arrows start end precisely measured box edges instead anchored box centers. Diagrams longer depend justification tricks fake edge attachment, connections stay correct device size. previous layers remain available geom_consort_box() geom_consort_arrow(). Boxes receive arrow longer shifted inferred justification; box entered left keeps position left-aligns ask hjust = 0. Set hjust/vjust explicitly old inference load-bearing.

title: ggconsort 0.2.0
text: consort_box_add() gains optional hjust vjust arguments set text justification individual box, overriding default inference connected arrows (#24). geom_consort_box() (therefore geom_consort()) now applies label_size, label_color, label_height arguments, box text can resized recolored, forwards ... ggtext::geom_richtext() (#18). cohort_count_adorn() formats counts comma separators default, e.g. "Randomized (n = 5,932,291)". Use .label_fn argument custom formats (#23). ggsave() now works directly ggconsort object via new grid.draw() method (#26). New pkgdown article showing build PRISMA 2020 flow diagram (#14).

title: ggconsort 0.2.0
text: create_consort_data() longer errors diagram arrows lines (#21, #22, thanks @jrealgatius). Arrows lines use linewidth instead size aesthetic deprecated ggplot2 3.4.0; ggconsort now requires ggplot2 >= 3.4.0 (#28). plot() ggconsort object now passes margin_h margin_v arguments theme_consort(). box several incoming arrows longer drawn per arrow; text justification inferred across entry sides. create_consort_data() also gives informative error called anything ggconsort object.

title: ggconsort 0.2.0
text: exported functions now documented, including consort_box_add(), consort_arrow_add(), consort_line_add(), geom_consort() family (#25). README reference examples consistently list arrow start arguments end arguments (#15).

title: ggconsort 0.1.0
text: Initial GitHub release.
